mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is falling from an already low level, discount-rate pressure eases and equity valuations re-rate upward. The signal is strongest for the next 20 trading days because markets transmit policy expectations faster than real activity data.
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS declines over the signal window but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of such cases, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate above a stress threshold signals weakening household income and future earnings risk, which should push S&P 500 forward returns negative over the next month as investors price slower demand and higher default risk.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations with UNRATE above 9.0% produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 55% of the time, or if the signal has no negative average forward return relative to untriggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous improvement in retail sales and industrial production indicates broad real-demand acceleration that supports earnings revisions and cyclical equity leadership. The joint effect should predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days, especially when both series are rising together.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined RSAFS-and-INDPRO signal does not outperform each series alone in out-of-sample Rank IC, or if positive 20-day returns do not occur in more than 55% of joint-acceleration episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent declines in industrial production reflect supply-side contraction and margin pressure, which should depress equity prices over the next quarter as analysts cut earnings estimates and cyclicals underperform.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward S&P 500 returns are not negative on average after three-month INDPRO declines, or if the triggered hit rate is not below 45% positive returns.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in housing starts after a prior slump signals improving interest-sensitive demand and construction activity, which lifts cyclical earnings expectations and supports positive equity returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises by at least 8% over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if housing-start rebounds fail to precede positive 20-day S&P 500 returns on average, or if the signal is not better than chance across triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll growth and retail sales both accelerate, real income and spending reinforce each other, creating a positive earnings impulse that should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect requires both labor income and consumption demand to improve together.
failure_condition: Reject if joint PAYEMS-RSAFS acceleration does not improve predictive power relative to each variable alone, or if average 20-day returns are not positive when both rise above their recent trend.

mechanism_narrative: During a labor-market deterioration phase, rising unemployment combined with payroll contraction signals recession risk and earnings downgrades. In that regime, S&P 500 forward returns should be negative over the next 60 trading days because equity investors reprice a weaker profit outlook.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint unemployment-plus-payroll stress signal does not produce negative 60-day returns in at least 55% of triggered cases, or if the interaction term is not statistically useful out of sample.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation is flat or falling while the policy rate remains extremely low, real-rate pressure eases and financial conditions stay supportive. This combination should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next week as investors extend risk exposure.
failure_condition: Reject if the low-rate/disinflation combination does not yield positive 5-day S&P 500 returns more often than 55% of the time, or if the interaction signal is not stronger than either proxy alone.
